summaries.Horpynchenko Maksym: Maksym worked extensively on the Mission Creator (MC) frontend application. His effort centred on mission-item lifecycle: enforcing TAKEOFF as the mandatory first item, preventing its replacement, implementing SYSTEM_MISSION_ITEMS (v1 and v2), and adding AZIMUTH: SMART TAKEOFF support. He improved UX by making Start Point behave like a Mission Item, adding waypoint indexing to Cover Area and the drawer, enabling start-point selection by map click, and fixing zoom resetting after point moves. Validation work covered latitude/longitude bounds checking, min/max/empty field validation, and ensuring all mission items are validated before execution. He also fixed broken dropdowns, reordered the command dropdown, added Ukrainian localisation, set sensible defaults (altitude, Acceptance Error, localization source inheritance), and applied visual polish including drone icon colour and cycle line styling.
summaries.Nadia Khasyshyn: Nadia worked across the detection, tracking, and OSD subsystems. On detection she improved horizon estimation during search mode using IMU data, added a fixed horizon offset, expanded cloud datasets with 17 April field data, and tuned the classifier on cloudy-sky datasets. Tracking improvements included cancelling tracking when the system is confident the object is not a vehicle, and handling anomalous bounding-box size jumps. OSD work covered adding outline and brightness improvements to dev OSD metrics, displaying position-hold start events, and parameterising OSD configuration for Азимут via GitHub Actions. She also fixed Godot error reporting (showing only flight-controller info on process errors), made critical-process health checks conditional on whether Азимут or Вартовий is active, and improved horizon detection during scanning.
summaries.Danylo Tomashy: Danylo focused on flight control reliability and onboard system integration. He resolved startup and stability issues with MAVProxy and migrated it to mavlink_router, fixed drone sinusoidal flight path deviations, and addressed gimbal-related tracking instability including abrupt gimbal turns and tracker drift during sharp rotations. On the perception side, he continued scanning-mission logic after gimbal turns with no detection, and worked on optimising image-save throughput to eliminate FPS drops. He also adapted the TOF-D rangefinder to the current codebase, benchmarked CPU load from libcamera-hello and GStreamer, and fixed file obfuscation errors in the system. Additional work included alerting when an optical-flow sensor is physically disconnected.
summaries.Bohdan Forostianyi: Bohdan concentrated on Азимут flight-control algorithms and wind-adaptive behaviour. He resolved a rangefinder detection failure, implemented logic to increase gimbal angle upon soft-limit detection, and reduced the Азимут dispersal radius to 30 m. His core focus was wind adaptation: auto-determining the initial maximum angle based on wind speed and azimuth, introducing deterministic azimuth correction over time, adding automatic max-angle adaptation when strong wind is detected, and embedding wind metadata for PID tuning. He also added a dedicated AZIMUTH HOLD stick, enabled POS HOLD to start with the current hold azimuth, and built mission-state adaptation to the current Азимут status.
summaries.Volodymyr Pavliukevych: Volodymyr spanned ML model development, OS/infrastructure, and tooling. On the model side he trained a new v0.7g detection model with sky improvements, distilled and trained a large YOLOv26L model as a reference tool, sped up training pipelines, and prepared a label tool for rapid tracker testing. He investigated and fixed a mavlink_router crash on the OS layer, resolved an OS update issue, and reviewed MAVLink listener tests related to ATP-1476. Additional work included building a drone emulator (ToDo: Emulator), investigating a tracker unexpected-liftoff incident, and implementing asset filtering from the autopilot.
summaries.Mr Lab: Mr Lab handled hardware and mechanical engineering tasks. Work included sourcing shielding solutions for USB cables, redesigning the VTX antenna mount for greater stability, creating a battery mount for the Вартовий drone, and 3D-printing additional landing legs for demonstration drones to improve antenna clearance. He also investigated a video signal loss incident on the Азимут drone, diagnosing why video dropped out during operation.
